description: Quality assurance for slide decks — layout validation, content lint, deck audit, and consistency checks. Use when the user says "is it good?", "audit my deck", "check for problems", "lint content", "check consistency", or "any issues?". Also use proactively after any conversion run. Catches overflow, empty slides, bullet overload, title issues, and cross-slide inconsistencies.

results.query: I just generated deck.pptx from the HTML slides. Can you check if there are any layout problems? Some text might be running off the edges
run validation on output/presentation.pptx and tell me the quality score
is this pptx any good? I want to know if elements are overflowing before I send it to my boss
audit my deck — give me the full quality breakdown across all categories
lint the content of my slides — check for bullet overload and title issues
check consistency across the deck — are the heading sizes uniform? any off-palette colors?
compare sales-q3.pptx and sales-q4.pptx for brand alignment and typography consistency
are there any problems with the slides? anything that needs fixing before presenting?
re-audit after I fixed the issues — show me the score delta
check my quotes — do they all have proper attribution with name and role?
how many of my slides have speaker notes? flag the ones that are missing them
I want to see what the PPTX looks like as images. Can you render it to PNGs?
create a contact sheet montage of all slides so I can get a quick overview
compare my HTML originals with the PPTX output side by side
convert these HTML slides to PowerPoint — just the conversion, no QA
run the full end-to-end pipeline: standardize, convert, validate, render, compare
the heading font is too small, can you make it 44pt instead of 36pt?
set the quality threshold to 90 for this project
standardize these AI-generated HTML files before I convert them
create a new presentation about machine learning trends from scratch
